title: Build Fluid Values with Hard Bounds
summary: Build and verify css math fluid sizing in survey heading and spacing fluidity review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css math fluid sizing from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css math fluid sizing without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Survey heading and spacing fluidity review case 1: fluid values need no minimum or maximum because every viewport scales proportionally.
Survey heading and spacing fluidity review case 1: write the minimum and maximum from usability constraints, build the preferred expression, and test every bound plus zoom and content changes.
Survey heading and spacing fluidity review case 1: use only viewport-width text so every line scales in exact proportion to the screen.

content.text: Min chooses the smallest candidate, max chooses the largest, clamp applies a minimum preferred value and maximum, and calc combines compatible values. Fluid values still need readable lower and upper bounds. The preferred expression may combine relative and viewport components, but zoom, long content, and extreme widths must not make text or controls unusable. Apply it to survey heading and spacing fluidity review.
